summary: Phase 3 backend testing (Orders + shipping + 10-product limit + loyalty + profile location sync). 18/18 pytest cases passed. Identified one critical RCA'd bug in /api/users/me/loyalty and one functional mismatch between order-loyalty and the loyalty status endpoint.

critical.issue: Uses entity.get('role') != 'user' but user entities have entity_type='user' and no 'role' field. Endpoint ALWAYS returns {points:0, tier:'none', is_user:False} for every real user regardless of actual loyalty. Confirmed via live call.

minor.issue: Orders increment users.loyalty_points (int(total)), but /api/users/me/loyalty computes points ONLY from completed bookings (10 per booking + capped spend). It ignores users.loyalty_points entirely. Review request explicitly says 'verify via GET /api/users/me/loyalty' but that cannot work today; verification currently only possible via /api/users/me (loyalty_points field).

'village' is stored in barber_profiles collection but not exposed in the enriched public response (only district/neighborhood/latitude/longitude are). Consumers cannot read the village saved through ProfileSetup LocationPicker.

action_items: Fix /api/users/me/loyalty: change entity.get('role') != 'user' to entity.get('entity_type') != 'user' (around server.py line 647). This alone unbreaks the endpoint.

Decide semantics of loyalty: either (a) add users.loyalty_points into base_points in /users/me/loyalty so order-awarded points are reflected, or (b) stop incrementing users.loyalty_points in create_order and document that loyalty is booking-driven only. Current setup awards silently and never surfaces it.
Expose 'village' field in enrich_barbershop_for_frontend (mirror the pattern used for neighborhood/district) so ProfileSetup → dashboard round-trip is lossless.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line ~647: `if entity.get('role') != 'user':` is a latent defect — token-based users have no 'role' key. Use 'entity_type'.

server.py create_order awards loyalty_points using int(total) with no cap and no multiplier, diverging from the booking-based loyalty tiers logic (10/booking + capped spend). Two parallel point systems now exist in the DB.
server.py line 3509 count = 3509: file is >700 LOC (actually ~3.5k). Recommend splitting into routers (products.py, orders.py, loyalty.py, barbers.py) for maintainability.

ORDER_STATUSES defined as a set but statuses like 'shipped' can currently be set on an order that was never 'confirmed' — no state-machine validation (any→any allowed). Consider linear state transitions.
/api/orders/{id}/cancel allows cancel right after status=shipped (only blocks delivered/cancelled). Business-wise, shipped orders probably shouldn't be cancellable by the customer.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 executed tests passed). Loyalty endpoint bug surfaced via supplemental live check, not via assertion.

test_credentials.note: Test users are created per-run with prefix TEST_<uuid>; password user123.

seed_data_creation: Each test run creates: 1 TEST_ product with all 3 shipping_options, 1 TEST_Pickup_ product (pickup-only), and up to 8 TEST_Fill_ products (auto-deleted after the 10-limit test). Also persists TEST_Al-Mazzeh/TEST_East Block/TEST_Dummar in the salon's location — main agent may want to reset the seed salon coordinates after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 Orders + Products limit + Profile location sync all verified working. The only real defect is the /api/users/me/loyalty role/entity_type check (and the semantic split between order-loyalty and booking-loyalty). Salon id 23a8effc-7e65-4869-9202-b1447430cf45 now has TEST_ products + location fields overwritten — reseed if needed. Frontend smoke tests were explicitly skipped per request (backend only).
rca of the issue: BUG: GET /api/users/me/loyalty returns {is_user:false, points:0} for all real users. REPRODUCE: register a user via POST /api/auth/register, call GET /api/users/me/loyalty → body={'points':0,'tier':'none','bookings_completed':0,'is_user':False}. ROOT CAUSE: server.py line ~647 uses `entity.get('role') != 'user'` but get_current_entity stores the type under key 'entity_type' (line 414). `role` is never set on user docs, so the guard clause always triggers. MITIGATION: replace with `entity.get('entity_type') != 'user'`. Additionally, orders award users.loyalty_points (int(total)) but this endpoint never reads that field — consider merging booking-based + order-based points into a single source of truth.
